stemHtml: What is the correct description of catalytic ozone converters and their use in aircraft?

options.a: Ozone is an inert gas. Ozone converters are fitted on some pressurised aircraft for the purpose of increasing the partial pressure of oxygen in the cabin by converting ozone into oxygen.
options.b: Ozone is mainly concentrated in the stratosphere. Aircraft flying in the stratosphere are fitted with converters that turn oxygen into ozone in order to increase the ozone layer.
options.c: Ozone is toxic and mainly concentrated in the stratosphere. Converters that reduce ozone to oxygen are normally fitted on aircraft flying in or near the stratosphere.
options.d: Ozone is toxic and concentrated at low flight levels in areas with strong atmospheric pollution and in the stratosphere. Converters that reduce ozone to oxygen are recommended for all aircraft and are standard on large, complex aircraft.
